quiz.title: Unit 10 Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Applications with Acceleration, Position, and Complex Rates (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Calculus

questions.question: A particle moves along a line with acceleration \(a(t) = 6t - 12\) meters per second squared. At \(t = 0\), the particle has velocity \(v(0) = 8\) m/s and position \(s(0) = 2\) meters. Find the net displacement from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 5\).

The rate at which a population grows is given by \(P'(t) = 1000 + 200\sin t - 300\cos t\) individuals per year. Find the net change in population from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 2\pi\). If \(P(0) = 5000\), find \(P(2\pi)\).

Water flows into a tank at a rate of \(r_{in}(t) = 20 + 5\sin t\) gallons per minute and out at a rate of \(r_{out}(t) = 10 + 3\cos t\) gallons per minute. Find the net change in volume from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 2\pi\). If the tank initially contains 100 gallons, find the volume at \(t = 2\pi\).
questions.explanation: Net rate = \(r_{in}(t) - r_{out}(t) = (20 + 5\sin t) - (10 + 3\cos t) = 10 + 5\sin t - 3\cos t\). Net change = \(\int_{0}^{2\pi} (10 + 5\sin t - 3\cos t) dt = [10t - 5\cos t - 3\sin t]_{0}^{2\pi} = (20\pi - 5\cos(2\pi) - 3\sin(2\pi)) - (0 - 5\cos 0 - 3\sin 0) = (20\pi - 5) - (-5) = 20\pi\) gallons. Volume at \(t = 2\pi\) = \(100 + 20\pi\) gallons.

questions.question: A car's velocity is given by \(v(t) = 3t^2 - 18t + 24\) meters per second. Find the net displacement from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 5\).
For the car in question 4, find the total distance traveled from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 5\).

The marginal revenue for a product is \(R'(x) = 200e^{-0.1x}\) dollars per unit, and the marginal cost is \(C'(x) = 50 + 2x\) dollars per unit. Find the net change in profit when production increases from 0 to 20 units.
A particle moves along a line with velocity \(v(t) = \frac{1}{t+1} - \frac{1}{2}\) meters per second for \(t \geq 0\). Find the net displacement from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 3\).
The acceleration of a particle is \(a(t) = 12t^2 - 24t\) m/s². At \(t = 0\), \(v(0) = 10\) m/s and \(s(0) = 0\) meters. Find the position function \(s(t)\).
A chemical reaction produces a gas at a rate of \(P'(t) = 4t e^{-t^2}\) liters per second. Find the net change in gas volume from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 2\) seconds.
The velocity of a particle is \(v(t) = e^{-t} \sin t\) meters per second. Find the net displacement from \(t = 0\) to \(t = \pi\).
questions.explanation: Using the given hint: \(\int e^{-t} \sin t dt = -\frac{e^{-t}(\sin t + \cos t)}{2} + C\). Then \(\int_{0}^{\pi} e^{-t} \sin t dt = [-\frac{e^{-t}(\sin t + \cos t)}{2}]_{0}^{\pi} = -\frac{e^{-\pi}(0 - 1)}{2} + \frac{e^{0}(0 + 1)}{2} = \frac{e^{-\pi}}{2} + \frac{1}{2} = \frac{1 + e^{-\pi}}{2}\) meters.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered advanced Net Change Theorem applications with acceleration, position, and complex rate functions.
scoreMessages.70: Excellent work! You handle challenging net change problems well. Review the ones involving total distance.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort! Keep practicing with position functions from acceleration and integration techniques.
scoreMessages.0: Review the relationship between position, velocity, and acceleration. Practice integrating to find net change and total distance.
